canonicalKinds.name: Command Injection
canonicalKinds.description: Untrusted input reaches a command execution sink without proper validation or allowlisting
canonicalKinds.agenticContext: Agentic systems often execute commands as a core capability. The vulnerability occurs when user-controlled or model-controlled input reaches the execution sink without a guard such as a command allowlist.

canonicalKinds.name: Path Traversal
canonicalKinds.description: Untrusted input controls file paths without workspace-root enforcement or path canonicalization
canonicalKinds.agenticContext: Agentic systems often read and write files as a core capability. The vulnerability occurs when user-controlled or model-controlled paths escape the intended workspace root.

canonicalKinds.name: Server-Side Request Forgery
canonicalKinds.description: Untrusted input controls outbound request targets without host or scheme restrictions
canonicalKinds.agenticContext: Agentic systems often make outbound network requests as a core capability. The vulnerability occurs when user-controlled or model-controlled URLs reach the request sink without a host allowlist or scheme restriction.

canonicalKinds.name: Authentication Bypass
canonicalKinds.description: Missing or insufficient caller identity verification allows unauthenticated access to protected endpoints or actions
canonicalKinds.agenticContext: Agentic systems expose control surfaces (HTTP servers, WebSocket handlers, webhooks) that must verify caller identity. The vulnerability occurs when identity checks are missing, use weak matching (e.g. empty-string bypass), or can be circumvented via encoding tricks.

canonicalKinds.name: Authorization Bypass
canonicalKinds.description: Authenticated callers can escalate privileges, access resources outside their granted scope, or bypass role restrictions
canonicalKinds.agenticContext: Agentic systems manage scoped permissions, device pairings, and channel bindings. The vulnerability occurs when scope checks are missing, scope tokens can be self-minted with broader privileges, or role boundaries are not enforced consistently.

canonicalKinds.name: SQL Injection
canonicalKinds.description: Untrusted input reaches SQL query construction without parameterization or proper escaping
canonicalKinds.agenticContext: Agentic systems with database nodes, import/export features, or dynamic query builders can pass user-controlled or model-controlled input into SQL queries. The vulnerability occurs when identifiers or values are string-interpolated rather than parameterized.
